reasoning: The Polymarket price has dropped significantly from 0.90 on March 30 to 0.53 on April 12, reflecting a stalled Russian advance and successful Ukrainian counterattacks in the Huliaipole sector. While Russia has captured numerous surrounding settlements, effectively enveloping the city from several sides, recent news indicates Ukrainian forces are "making progress" and counterattacking, creating contested (grey) zones. The resolution criteria strictly require the entirety of the municipality to be shaded red on the ISW map. With only 18 days left until the April 30 deadline, achieving and maintaining 100% undisputed mapped control amidst an active Ukrainian counter-operation is unlikely, though a rapid collapse of the pocket remains a possibility (which keeps the probability elevated near 40-45%). I am discounting the Polymarket 53% slightly due to the strictness of the 100%-red mapped requirement and the lagging nature of ISW map updates.
